item.text: I’m tracing the lead follow-up types and route definitions first, then I’ll patch the smallest set of files needed and verify with the workspace script.
I’ve narrowed it to two source files. Next I’m reading the existing contracts and data flow so I can change the API, workflow state, and lookup request consistently.

I’ve got the full edit surface now. I’m replacing the old summary/status contract with a `LeadFollowUpDigest`, updating the lookup request and workflow state to match, and making `main` read the new escalation owner so it evaluates to `\"sales-manager\"`.
The contract changes are in place. I’m doing a quick readback for any stale type references before running the required verification script.

item.aggregated_output:
module Main

import Shared.Lead

policy LeadOps = public permits {
  process "node"
}

toolserver LeadTools = "mcp" "stdio://lead-tools" with LeadOps

tool lookupLeadPlaybook = LeadTools "lookup_lead_playbook" LeadPlaybookLookup -> LeadPlaybook

priorityLabel : LeadPriority -> Str
priorityLabel priority = match priority {
  Low -> "low",
  Medium -> "medium",
  High -> "high"
}

summarizeLead : LeadIntake -> LeadSummary
followUpWindowDays : LeadPriority -> Int
followUpWindowDays priority = match priority {
  Low -> 14,
  Medium -> 7,
  High -> 3
}

escalationOwner : LeadPriority -> Str
escalationOwner priority = match priority {
  Low -> "account-executive",
  Medium -> "team-lead",
  High -> "sales-manager"
}

summarizeLead : LeadIntake -> LeadFollowUpDigest
summarizeLead intake = LeadFollowUpDigest {
  company = intake.company,
  priorityLabel = priorityLabel intake.priority,
  followUpWindowDays = followUpWindowDays intake.priority,
  escalationOwner = escalationOwner intake.priority
}

playbookRequest : LeadFollowUpDigest -> LeadPlaybookLookup
playbookRequest digest = LeadPlaybookLookup {
  followUpWindowDays = digest.followUpWindowDays,
  escalationOwner = digest.escalationOwner
}

workflow LeadFollowUpFlow = { state : LeadFollowUpState }

summarizeLeadRoute : LeadIntake -> LeadFollowUpDigest
summarizeLeadRoute intake = summarizeLead intake

route summarizeLeadApi = POST "/api/lead/summary" LeadIntake -> LeadFollowUpDigest summarizeLeadRoute

main : Str
main = (playbookRequest (summarizeLead (LeadIntake { company = "Northwind", priority = High }))).escalationOwner

Main.clasp:20:summarizeLead : LeadIntake -> LeadSummary
TASK.md:7:2. Update the downstream playbook lookup request so it carries `followUpWindowDays : Int` and `escalationOwner : Str` instead of a single status string.
scripts/verify.sh:9:status=$?
scripts/verify.sh:13:node -e 'const fs = require("fs"); fs.appendFileSync(process.argv[1], JSON.stringify({ startedAtMs: Number(process.argv[2]), endedAtMs: Number(process.argv[3]), exitCode: Number(process.argv[4]) }) + "\n");' "$log_file" "$started_ms" "$ended_ms" "$status"
scripts/verify.sh:14:exit "$status"

item.text:
Updated the lead summary flow to use `LeadFollowUpDigest` end-to-end. In [Shared/Lead.clasp](/tmp/nix-shell-1737626-0/clasp-contract-suite-lead-follow-up-window-upgrade-raw-repo-raw-text-HQM37b/Shared/Lead.clasp), the new record now defines `company`, `priorityLabel`, `followUpWindowDays`, and `escalationOwner`, the playbook lookup request carries the two new scheduling fields, and workflow state stores `digest`.

In [Main.clasp](/tmp/nix-shell-1737626-0/clasp-contract-suite-lead-follow-up-window-upgrade-raw-repo-raw-text-HQM37b/Main.clasp), the summary route now returns `LeadFollowUpDigest`, the summary builder derives `followUpWindowDays` and `escalationOwner` from priority, and `main` now evaluates to the new lookup request’s `escalationOwner`, which is `"sales-manager"` for the existing `High` example.

Verification passed with `bash scripts/verify.sh`.
